title: Vintage Toddler Bed
summary: A low toddler bed with paneled headboard and footboard ends framed in 1x3/1x6, plywood or beadboard panel inserts, and optional turned sofa feet for a classic vintage look.
description:
Each end of the bed is a small frame-and-panel assembly — 1x3 and 1x6 boards frame a plywood or beadboard panel insert, giving the headboard and footboard real dimensional detail instead of a flat board. Build both ends identically, then join them with the long 1x8 side rails that carry the mattress slats.

**The 2x2 legs come in two lengths depending on your choice of feet** — use the taller cut if the bed sits directly on the legs, or the shorter cut if turned sofa feet with metal brackets are being added, since the sofa feet themselves make up the difference in height. Fifteen 1x3 slats span the side rails to support the mattress — check spacing against your actual mattress before finalizing slat count.

This is a genuinely low bed, sized for a toddler transitioning out of a crib, not a scaled-down adult bed frame — keep the low profile in mind when choosing a finish, since it will get climbed on and over constantly.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood or beadboard, at least 25-1/4 in x 27-1/2 in
Turned sofa feet with metal bracket, 6 in (optional)
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood or beadboard, at least 25-1/4 in x 27-1/2 in

cutList.note: Use 16-1/2" instead if adding turned sofa feet.

steps.title: Build the end panels
steps.body: Frame the plywood or beadboard panels with the 1x3 and 1x6 boards to build the headboard and footboard ends.
materials: 1/4" plywood or beadboard, at least 25-1/4 in x 27-1/2 in

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to each end panel, choosing the taller or shorter cut depending on whether turned sofa feet are being added.
materials: Turned sofa feet with metal bracket, 6 in (optional)

steps.title: Join the ends with the side rails
steps.body: Connect the two end assemblies with the side rails and long side cleats.

steps.title: Add the slats
steps.body: Attach the slat cleats, then lay the fifteen slats across, adjusting spacing to your own mattress.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Vintage style toddler bed with paneled ends
